author: @techbluejay
text:
The tech review world is a swamp of unreliable content, and you and MKBHD were the ones who stood out. MOSTLY YOU. Your deep passion, meticulous attention to detail, and fiercely unbiased deep dives always made your content a priority watch for me. That's why this recent video was such a jarring departure that I couldn't even finish it.

I'm writing to share my honest feedback on your iphone air / 17 lineup video. From the get-go, it felt less like a review and more like a fawning piece of Apple propaganda. The bias was so blatant it was uncomfortable to watch. For the record, I don’t hate Apple; I'm typing this email on an Apple device right now unfortunately.

Many of the features presented as revolutionary have been standard for years in the Android ecosystem, and this is not news which we all know. But the way you delivered it today made a huge gap and hurt me. The moment that sealed it for me, however, was when you casually tossed out a "20% performance upgrade" claim without any of your signature context, data, or critical analysis. It was a frustratingly shallow comment, completely uncharacteristic of the depth you're known for.

To be completely transparent, the video felt like a soulless, sponsored piece rather than the objective analysis I've come to expect. The opinions seemed disingenuous, and it lost the core authenticity that made me (and clearly, many others) subscribe in the first place.

This feedback comes from a place of genuine respect for what your work used to represent. The reason your channel became so successful is because you felt like an authentic, trusted voice in the tech space. Even if it were your opinion, I wanted to hear it, listen to your opinion, LEARN from you. Now, I just wanted to share that for a long-time viewer, this particular video felt like it was missing that critical, unbiased spark entirely.

No, I don’t write emails like this. Just imagine how profoundly your latest iPhone 17 Air video affected me to prompt something I've never done before. I geniunely felt hurt.

Thank you for taking the time to read this. I'm not sure if I have the heart to follow ANY YouTubers including you for tech reviews anymore. I'm wishing you a goodbye and good luck for your future.

author: @Bruntygenius
text: Sorry I will be having both. I currently run the 16 Pro Max as my personal phone. On Upgrade Anytime so will be upgrading to the 17 Pro Max (Changing from 1Tb to 2tb) in Orange. I am also running a 15 Plus as my work phone. When I am at my desk this is attached using the MagSafe stand to the top of my monitor using Continuity Camera for work calls. The contract has run out so I'm due an upgrade. I like the bigger screen so the 17 is a bit too small for me, but I don't need the Pro Max as a work phone. The Air is a good middle ground, the screen is bigger than the 17, the weight reduction is great when it needs to sit on my monitor as it more stable. I don't need the extra long battery life or extra cameras as they are not used. I wouldn't have it as my main phone but it will work great as a work phone for me.

author: @victorsvensson7914
text: Pro motion is the feature where the screen always have the same screen framerate as the media source frame rate. So 24 hz for film, 30 hz for the most source material and 60 hz somtimes on youtube videos that did record in 60 fps, 120 only for some games. This is important because if the screen framerate has higher frame rate than the source media, then you will see stutter/jitter/ and not smooth motion. You see this especially well when a camera is panning, or if the screen is big. I see this all the time on my 27 inch 4 K computer screen. And because the screen does not have motionflow as Sony TV:s or Xmotion or Truemotion as Samsung and LG:s TV:s I need software to do framerate interpolation when watching videos on youtube or elsewhere, so I do not see this jitter and stutter. That is why I always bought the iPhone Pro Max, because you dont see this sutter/jitter. There are several website where you can see the stutter effect when measuing frame rate. Do a search for it and you will then see the stutter effect on all screens that is not an iPhone. You will hate every other phone and computer screen.

author: @Josepizza2000-3
text:
The standard iPhone 18 is reportedly delayed, with a release date expected in Spring 2027 in the US, while the higher-end iPhone 18 Pro, Pro Max, and Air models are anticipated for a September 2026 launch. This is a shift from Apple's typical annual September release schedule for the entire iPhone lineup. 

Why the Change?

Foldable Phone Focus: 

This change is reportedly to maximize the impact of Apple's rumored first foldable iPhone, which is expected to launch in Fall 2026. 

Spreading Out Launches: 

The strategy is to spread out the launch of different iPhone tiers, releasing the premium models in the fall and the more affordable models in the spring of the following year. 

What to Expect

Fall 2026: 

You can expect the first foldable iPhone, along with the iPhone 18 Pro, iPhone 18 Pro Max, and iPhone 18 Air to be released. 

Spring 2027: 

The standard iPhone 18 and the entry-level iPhone 18e are slated for release in Spring 2027.

author: @ChristopherJRoura
text:
@Mrwhosetheboss The iPhone Air’s slimness isn’t just a design tweak, it’s a feature. One that I think people will crave and want. From years fixing phones, I’ve seen how much thickness matters: a thinner phone slips into pockets or purses without bulging, which many users (especially women) value. It’s the same appeal behind the Z Flip with form factor and portability drive being the demand even when specs aren’t top-tier. Apple has a history of making thinness a product category (MacBook Air, iPad Air), and unless they can compress all Pro features into this ultra-slim frame, which feels years away, the Air has a clear niche and real staying power. 

Love your opinions on everything though and love the content too. Just wanted to shine some extra light on this topic having the experience.

author: @Ryan48093
text: Thin bezels and anti-glare coatings on phones are a joke. Not one manufacturer makes a display that can survive coins in a pocket or keys in a purse without scratching, which keeps screen protectors necessary. The problem is, screen protectors don’t work well with thin bezels. They either cover part of the display with a black border, leave edges exposed, or fail to fully adhere to the edge. And once you put one on, you completely kill whatever anti-glare benefit the phone had in the first place.

author: @hasibulhasan4224
text: Apple's iPhone 17 Air reveal has sparked some serious side-eye. An orange phone? Bold, but if any other company tried this, it’d be a flop faster than you can say "niche." The gripe is that Apple’s serving reheated ideas with zero spice—same old recipe, just with their shiny logo slapped on. Whispers are circulating that the Air is a near-carbon copy of Tecno’s Slim phone, which has people questioning if Apple’s running on fumes creatively. Yet, the cash keeps rolling in, fueled by die-hard fans who’d buy an Apple-branded brick. Disappointing? For sure, if you’re craving real innovation.

author: @ApfelSgt
text:
Edit: LONG comment! Sorry for that! 🤣 But i had to write my frustration with Apple down…

Seems like a great phone! I like the new look and performance seems like to be great too! 
But still… no new innovations…. Still no Ai, which is the new and probably most important thing on smartphones! Apple really is SO MUCH BEHIND in this case and if they don’t do anything BIG in terms of AI the next couple of months, it will probably start the downfall of the iphone! 🤦🏽‍♂️ 

I was a Android Fan for a very long time. Then i first tried Apple with the Iphone 11 Pro Max when it came out. I absolutely loved it and thought i will never go back! 
But so much happened the last years and i am more and more disappointed with apple…. I currently have the iphone 15 pro max and for the last 2 years i really started to think about buying an android again! It’s because slowly but steadily iPhones start to get boring to me! 
Android smartphones are constantly evolving and bringing new ideas and innovations meanwhile with apple absolutely NOTHING happens! 
I mean, between the iphone 11 pro and the 13 pro there was the design change as well as 120hz and the camera got a lot better. These were the last great things that they changed…. 
But between the 13 pro max and the 15 pro max? Felt absolutely the same! Nearly nothing changed and in my honest opinion i was even more happy with the 13 pro max than with the 15 pro max because the battery life was WAY BETTER with the 13 pro max! 🤦🏽‍♂️ 
Since the iphone 11 pro, which was 6 years ago… nearly nothing happened! 
And since the 13 pro which is 4 years ago they’re nearly identical! 😒 
The only thing that happens is the cameras getting a tiny bit better and they get more powerful… which both basically isn’t noticeable because smartphones are fast enough for all kind of things for years now! And the cameras are so good since years too, so that you could only notice it in a direct comparison… 

The reason i switched from android to iOS was that android phones simply were worse back then. The hardware was A LOT slower, they were badly optimised, they had lots of freezes and bugs, they only got 2 years of software updates at highest, the cameras mostly were worse and face ID was way ahead! 

But things have changed A LOT since then! And basically every point where android phones were way worse they’re now just as good or even BETTER! 🤷🏽 
The new snapdragons are more powerful in CPU and GPU than iPhones „A“ chips are. Could be possible that the A19 pro is better again, but i am pretty sure the new snapdragon will overtake  Them by a lot and raise the bar to a point where apple couldn’t keep up again like it was the past few years! 😒 
Android phones are well optimised these days and probably have less bugs and freezes than iOS! Because android got much better over the last couple of years while iOS constantly becomes worse with every new version! With my iphone 11 pro max i didn’t got ONE SINGLE crash or freeze or bug or anything over the 2 years! With the 13 pro max i got a few and with my iPhone 15 pro max i got a lot over the 2 years! I would even say i had more crashes or bugs in the first few months than i had with my 11 pro max and 13 pro max over the full 4 years COMBINED! And with every new iOS it gets worse instead of getting better… 
Also Android phones didn’t even tie it with software updates, NO…. They overtook Apple! You get updates for a LONGER time! 🤦🏽‍♂️
On some phones the cameras also are better or at least the same like with iphone! And Face ID also is absolutely nothing special anymore that is so much superior… 

But on top of all those things, Android got so much nice features and could do so much things that iOS can’t! 
And you got Foldables FOR YEARS now and they got So damn good these days! 
I want a foldable so badly and would instantly buy one, but apple still don’t have one… WHY Apple ?! I simply don’t get it?! 😤

AND most importantly… the biggest and probably only real „innovation“ smartphones got since i would say A DECADE is AI! And Android phones are simply MILES ahead with it! 
iPhones always were and felt „modern“…. Since the last 2 years having an iphone feels kinda old! Like back when smartphones came out and you had a „button cellphone“! 
And i am highly disappointed not only that apple lied so much about their AI, but also that even with the NEW iPhone, they still don’t care about it and nearly didn’t even talk about it! 😤
It kinda reminds me of the mistakes of other market leaders like Nokia back then, where they completely lost their customers and relevancy in the market! 
If you don’t try to innovate it’s okay as long as you’re the best in this business! BUT when others start to overtake you more and more, then you have to innovate or you will lose the fight and your customers! 🤦🏽‍♂️

I already would switch to Android, if i weren’t trapped in the apple ecosystem! 🤷🏽 I have a mac boob (which i love), i have a apple watch, i have Air Pod pros and i have a Ipad Pro… 
Every 2 years i buy a new phone, so i will buy the 17 pro Max again even when i am again disappointed from the „new“ iPhone! 😪 

BUT, this will be the last chance that i give apple! If the Iphone 18 pro max and than the 19 pro max will be the same again with no big changes and innovations, i will 100% switch to android and i am 100% done with apple! Regardless if I then can’t use my phone well with my other apple products! 
I am tired from paying ~1500€ for getting the same over and over again and not even getting the new technology which is AI…. Android phones have SO MUCH amazing features that i saw with AI and apple could do nearly nothing that’s useful! 😤 It’s the first time since i own iPhones that i am jealous that i don’t have an Android smartphone… that’s a sign that apple is losing me as a customer and i am pretty sure it’s the same for many apple fans!  

I really wanted to see something new with the 17 pro! It’s basically the same like the 16 pro just with a new design… the only thing that i hope but doubt will be a big upgrade are the cameras! 
I truly believe the pictures don’t look much different…

 I hoped so much that they will include a nice Telephoto! Cameras are overall great since years, but with zoom, they are just so much worse than some android phones! That literally is the only thing i wished they will do… but instead of doing that, they decreased the telephoto from 5x to 4x! 🤦🏽‍♂️🤬
Yes, i know, it’s a bigger and better sensor that will probably still have the better zoom and eventually 8x with digital zoom will look as good as the 5x from the iphone 16 pro… 
But still! A Digital zoom is ALWAYS worse than a optical! 
I would be absolutely happy with a 10x or even a 8x telephoto! But 4x in 2025?! Common apple… 🤦🏽‍♂️ 

They changed the design, they made the phone thicker, they made the camera bump bigger…. If they already made that, they really could have changed the triangle camera design too and make it 4 cameras! A 3x which is perfect for things like portraits and a 8x or 10x for good zooming and be competitive with Samsung! 
That would‘ve been amazing! 

I truly hope that when i have the 17 pro max that it will surprise me and i will love it! But i highly doubt it! I believe it will feel and basically be the same smartphone than my 15 pro max and even my 13 pro max was and that i again  will tell myself „why have you bought this thing?!“ 😓

author: @rocco-yq1js
text:
I am happy with the iPhone Air and will definitely trade in my 16Pro for an iPhone Air. I find it perfect for avoiding the slavery of social media scrolling. The “penalized” camera is more than enough for immediate use, but when I want to take photos, I'll use a DLSR, which will allow me to focus on photography without interruptions from notifications and without the urgency of quickly editing on my smartphone to immediately post the photo on social media and then remain “enchanted” waiting for reactions or looking at recent posts. The single speaker is also a blessing because it is always polite to use earphones instead of deafening others with your music, podcasts, or whatever else. I find myself using the speakers mainly at home, but having a HomePod easily solves the problem. Cars now all have Bluetooth systems, and in the gym or other social gathering places, I find using speakers to be a serious form of rudeness. So, in the end, I believe that the iPhone Air restores a certain balance in cell phone “addiction” and imposes a return to more socially ethical behavior.
Maybe there's still a chance that there are people who are happy to have more time their hands on a book, a pen, even their dick or someone else dick! Instead of their smartphone. Thank you, Apple.

author: @Nifibizgaming24
text:
Hey mrwhosetheboss, I don’t usually write stuff like this, but I’ve been watching you for about 3 years now and just felt like saying something. Your videos have honestly helped me through a lot. Gaming's always been something I’ve loved, but lately it’s been kind of hard to enjoy it the same way. Most of my friends have these amazing setups — like full-on PCs with all the gear — and I’m still working with a really basic setup that can barely keep up. They don’t always say stuff directly, but the little jokes or looks when we play together kind of make me feel like I don’t belong or I’m not taken seriously just because I don’t have the same kind of gear. It gets to me sometimes, even if I try not to let it.

The thing is, watching your content reminds me why I started gaming in the first place. It’s not about how good your setup is — it’s about enjoying the game, having fun, and being part of something. You make it feel like that again. I know this might sound random or even a bit much, but if there was ever a chance you could help me get a PC or anything like that, it would seriously change everything for me. I get that you probably get tons of messages like this, so no worries if not — I just really wanted to say thank you either way. You’ve made a bigger difference in my life than you probably know. and lastly jesus is lord the kinggs of kings the lord or lord all glory to him only

I have a 16 pro max and a s24 ultra and iQOO neo 6, I don't feel good while using an iPhone while on Android it's Just amazing.

author: @davids6461326
text:
So this year we’ve got the iPhone 17 Air – ultra-thin, sleek, but to actually make a call, you need to buy the MagSafe powerbank 😎📱. Don’t worry though, Apple is already working on the next hit: iPhone 18 Air! Imagine this – a phone so thin that it’s basically just a display. All the components? You attach them via MagSafe LEGO-style – battery here, processor there, camera separately, maybe even the speakers on their own modules!
Phone price? A symbolic $999. Price of the extra components? Another $999 😏💸. But in return… a super-thin phone you can actually use as a bookmark for your book 📖. Perfect for reading in a café and showing off to friends that you’re ultra-modern, while also saving the planet 🌍. Because the thinner the phone, the fewer materials, less plastic, less everything – basically eco-level: genius!
Picture this: sitting in the park, latte in one hand, book in the other… and that bookmark? Actually your iPhone 18 Air 😎. Minimalism, style, and eco-friendliness all in one – Apple level: genius.

author: @NikolaFilipovic
text:
No, you don't need back of the phone to be metal for effective heat dissipation. Samsung uses back glass and Titanium and it still has ZERO overheating issues. It's just poor thermal design on Apple's side. And, instead of redesigning insides, they are brute forcing it with more metal.
Samsung doesn't overheat when charging because they use a modern protocol called PPS that dynamically controls current being delivered, so phone never gets more than what it needs, so it doesn't need to dissipate the excess as heat.

author: @ZombieCoder
text: One of the best phones I have ever purchased was the N-Gage, running S60.  Nokia's phone offered a phone, but also a gaming joystick and SDK where you could write games in C and deploy them to the SSD card. I have an extremely nerdy and specific piece of feedback for this video. 😂 I can tell that you have a noise gate on your voice but you’re in a space with a lot of room reverb. This means that the moments when you stop speaking and it goes below the decible threshold, the audio cuts off suddenly and jarringly. It kept making me think the video paused. If you increase the attack on the gate or use something like Izotope RX de-verb to reduce the room reverb it would help.

Anyway - great video. Haha

author: @disclaimer4211
text:
Honestly I feel like the price-tag of the new Air in the lineup makes this thing feel unappealing at first glance, especially if apple was expecting you to buy the external magsafe battery bank anyway, like if I wanted to spend all that money, I'd go all in on the pro and get more for my money that way and still get the better battery life too... and you are only getting 1 camera on a thousand dollar phone? c'mon Apple.

Like don't get me wrong this thing looks absolutely beautiful, we haven't gotten something insane looking like this since the iPhone X, but somehow, I feel like Apple is repeating a mistake from the past like with the X.
They should've lowered the price of the base iPhone 17 to 699, given us the air for 899 and the keep the base pro for price. 
I really hope the Air doesn't flop like the iPhone mini, because it looks insane, even if Apple didn't need to do this. 
I actually think ppl should go out and buy it, but for 999 it feels a little steep considering what's around that corner if you just spend a little more.

author: @Slider93
text: I'm glad they changed obnoxious 5x zoom to 4x zoom. 4x zoom is still completely pointless but it's slightly more usable. The reason why it's obnoxiously useless is because your range from 1x to 3.9x is gonna a crop from the main camera. And it's a giant crop to make. 2.5x is the most reasonable one to use, because you can crop 2.5x with hybrid and get more or less usable picture, then switch to new lens and get another usable 2.5x crop which would be 6.25x zoom in total which is around 135. Yeah you lose quality in the 1.5-2.5x range very rapidly even with "hybrid zoom" tech but it's just a million times better than 4x zoom. There is a reason why "standard" focal range is 24-70.

text:
The last Samsung I had was the S10 Note. It was a buy based off of my S10e. I loved the e. It wanted an S pen. The OG buds were sick though!

I stopped trusting Samsung PHONES after the Note and e. Imagine charging the same amount of £ as $ when the £ was much stronger. For an inferior chip… madness. 

I do have a microwave, TV, and washing machine by them though. I don’t understand anyone who says “fan boy”. It’s a fuckin brand, buy what you like but my loyalty lies in what I believe is best value according to my needs.

author: @quantumuniverse4606
text:
Hey Arun! Big fan of your channel 👋. I wanted to share an idea that could be a great video topic: square camera sensors.

Apple (iPhone 17 Pro selfie) and DJI are already trying this — and if Sony/Canon/Nikon brought square sensors to their main cameras, it could change everything for creators.

Why it matters:
	•	Creators need horizontal + vertical equally (YouTube & TikTok).
	•	A square open-gate sensor = capture once, crop later (no reshooting/rotating).
	•	More creative flexibility in post without losing resolution.
	•	Big opportunity for camera brands — better use of lenses’ circular projection, no need for all-new lenses.

I haven’t seen anyone explain this yet, and your channel feels perfect for it. Would love to see your take on whether square sensors could be the next big thing!

author: @barneshudson
text:
The quality of a smartphone should not be measured solely by its thickness or by the trendy color of the year. We are talking here about an iPhone costing over a thousand dollars – without any visible features that could possibly justify the exorbitant price. Inevitably, the argument will be made that if you dare to philosophize about the price or question it at all, then you simply cannot afford the product. But have we really reached the point where we – like in The Emperor’s New Clothes – blindly accept any price, just because it carries the iPhone label?
The true value of a brand should once again be defined by quality and innovation. The color of a smartphone usually disappears beneath a protective case, which also makes discussions about tenths of a millimeter in thickness completely irrelevant. Whether the design of the “new” iOS now appears in fancy blue or green, or whether it is dressed up in the hyped “Liquid Glass,” it offers no real added value to me. It is nothing more than a facelift for an aging product – presented with a flashy marketing-branding spin. In the end, it remains nothing but a rip-off.

text:
i like this test apple is doing. making the pro phones more function over form, and providing a fer much form over function iphone while also giving people a good middle ground value option, they're no longer trying to split people into buying the pro phones to get a more "premium" experience. now if you want an iphone, you get the iphone 17. if you want a premium phone and care more about looks and feel than raw features, get the air. and if you dont care about the looks or feel and just want maximum features and function over form in an iphone, you get the pro. but you gotta pay in terms of price, and appearance.

author: @Modikie
text:
i was initially put off by the airs magsafe battery bank. however, i dont see it as "use this to get more battery life if you need more". i see this as "your battery life is going to be adequate for every day usage, same as the 16 pro. but if you are going on a road trip, and you need extra long battery life, use this battery pack and it makes it better than any phone you can get in this price range".

sometimes it isnt about the face value, but the actual real world use case implications. if the air had an actually horrible battery life so that the only way you could actually get through the day was with the magsafe battery pack, yeah we can clown on them for this. but the battery fits in line with the 16 pro battery life, and thats not bat AT ALL considering how incredibly thin this phone is. and if you have a long day where you're gonna need it, you can pop on the magsafe battery and be good for 2 days.

author: @maxonite
text:
You cannot seriously tell me that thinner bezels make ANY difference to ones phone at this point. They’re long past obstructive or a waste of space, so they are basically invisible already. I do not perceive them whatsoever, I cannot remember ever looking at my iPhone 15 and thinking "these bezels sure are ugly"
Bezels shrinking by the quarter of a millimeter every year have basically become representative of the smartphone industry as a whole - there is nothing left to innovate about this pocket computer. Buying a new phone is becoming more and more like buying a new fridge or a new toaster - a replacement for when the one you own stops working. And Apple knows this, so they desperately try to make it seem like this years iphone is actually different to the last. Slightly bigger camera lenses, slightly smaller bezels, but never an actual leap, because then they wouldn’t have anything new to sell the next year.

author: @Fireinthedark8
text:
Not at all they are adapting to the economy that’s what it is, but in the same time I think they are simply not making the products people want or are interested in.
These price drops go along with a big decrease in iPhone sales compared to other years, this has been the trend for the past 5-8 years and so they are making their new line ups more affordable in order to sell more volumes but what they don’t understand is that people don’t like the choices of design, neither do people need a 48mp camera for taking fking snap shots, and they don’t care about an A19 chip for gaming at 120fps on a tiny screen neither, it’s not comfortable and mobile games suck (apart from Fortnite).

People interested in videography or photography will pick an analogue camera or a dslr, and same for gaming people with pick a console or a pc, and then everything is more comfortable to do on an iPad next to an iPhone.

I personally use my iPhone as an extension of my iPad, not the other way around, and it’s the same thing for everybody who owns a pc or a MacBook. 
iPhones are used for messages and phone calls ffs, that’s what a phone is about and that’s what people do and use an iPhone primarily and while it’s nice to be able to read your emails and scroll through an app for entertainment quickly while on the go, nobody needs this kind of overpowered tech inside of a screen that fits in the pocket because you have to be outside of your home.

Even at this price tag I wouldn’t put my money on it simply because I have no use of it for I prefer to do gaming on a pc, be on internet, watch movies and videos on my iPad, and when it’s time to take pictures I will use one of my analogue cameras, so why do I need this kind of overpowered phone for exactly ? For no reason.

author: @Eye_of_Samadhi
text: Why are you claiming megapixel upgrades automatically equate to better quality photos ? In this particular instance it may well be the case but until there's an in depth camera review & photo comparison with the iPhone 16 it's probably best to hold off on such claims because any phone reviewer worth his salt knows megapixel upgrades aren't everything (post processing can create significant differences for example). From the critics perspective when you attempt to claim otherwise without the data/evidence to back it up it appears as if you're trying to take advantage of many Apple fans naivety regarding technical specifications & what they really mean which may manipulate & entice them towards an upgrade which may turn out to be completely unnecessary regarding their overall needs & expectations however it would conveniently assist Apple in realising their iPhone 17 sale targets/goals now wouldn't it ? I would expect this type of megapixel claim from an Apple salesman's but not from a legitimate unbiased phone reviewer who knows what they're talking about & who is apparently not sponsored. Reminder : if you've been incentivised by Apple in any way shape or form it is still classified as (indirect) sponsorship & you STILL have an obligation to disclose this to your audience ! Secret meetings, phonecalls &/or covert deals doesn't negate the need to disclose !

author: @Eye_of_Samadhi
text: Pretty sure you'll be able to select & lock on 60hz only if you wish to save battery. I've been using various 120hz capable devices since 2020 & their settings usually consist of 60hz, (sometimes 90hz also) 120hz & Auto which will choose the appropriate refresh rate depending on the content you're viewing. For example Auto will switch to 120hz while gaming then automatically dial back to 60hz for static content or if it's LTPO tech it'll dial all the way back to 1hz for static content. You can also lock it on 90hz or 120hz for ALL content but most people I know use the Auto function & let the phone do it's thing, balancing between saving battery life & optimising the user experience.

author: @scootndute579
text:
Just a heads-up: under the FTC’s Endorsement Guidelines (16 CFR Part 255), creators are legally required to clearly and conspicuously disclose when content includes a paid sponsorship, advertisement, or affiliate relationship. Failure to do so can be considered deceptive advertising under U.S. law.

Not labeling this segment as sponsored is a violation of disclosure requirements and could subject both the creator and the brand to FTC enforcement or penalties. Transparency matters—viewers have the right to know when they’re being advertised to.

author: @boatemaa08
text:
My take is this year line up is a let-down.
Battery & overheating: Biggest issue? Drains faster, gets hot even on light use. For this price, stability should be non-negotiable.
Design over function: Camera bar + colour tweaks (no black option?!) feel like gimmicks instead of real improvements.
Incremental upgrades: Chip and camera got better, but day-to-day iOS still lacks the small fixes that would actually make a difference.
What users really wanted: Smarter battery optimisation, cooler thermals, and app tweaks that improve daily experience not just another design experiment.
Bottom line: looks new, works worse. Apple focused on flash instead of fundamentals.

text: ​@paperplane-b6kmobile raytracing has been around since the snapdragon 8 gen 2 in 2023. Even then, there's still only like 3 games out there that can actually support hardware accelerated RT so it's a gimmick at best. What apps take advantage of raytracing on a phone compared to a laptop or gaming pc which has support for both a huge library of RT games and 3d animation software like blender? All of this without taking into account that most phones (androids and iPhones) running RT workloads will thermal throttle anyway and lose a ton of performance unlike an actual gaming or workstation rig

author: @prepperjonpnw6482
text:
The one thing I wish all phones had is at least one actual SIM card! 

I hate eSIMs so much! 😮

I travel a lot and being able to swap out a physical SIM card while I’m on the plane ✈️ 
so I can have service the moment I step foot off the plane is fantastic! 🎉

I don’t use those travel eSIMs because they are FAR more expensive than simply having a local phone number and they are a hassle. 

For example, when I go to the Philippines, I change my SIM card before landing in Manila or Clarke. 
I add some load to my phone and BAM!! I’m good to go! 🎉

I pay about $8usd a month for unlimited calls/texts and 30 Gbs of data. 
I can do that because I have a local SIM card. 

I do the same thing whether I’m in Japan, Thailand, Taiwan , or Vietnam. 

For anyone who wants to comment, “what if you lose the SIM card?” 
I can get a new one for less than $1usd and I don’t care about the actual phone number because I mainly use messenger and can quickly update everyone of my new number when needed. 

PLEASE bring back physical SIM cards!! ❤🎉

text:
the PIP front camera when showing rear camera will be a HUGE gamechanger for livestreamers who do IRL streams. Keep your eye on Twitch's IRL category for this feature being used by EVERYONE. Literally no longer having to switch cams to show stuff, with the momentary video cutoff it causes, yeah, a lot of people are going to love this.

Then there's techtubers like yourself who love to go to conventions and record what's going on: you might not realise it yet, but you WILL end up using this, and you'll be shocked you didn't think of it sooner and won't be able to go without it going forward.

Other thing the new iPhones fix for streamers: the unibody and vapour chamber. Streamers CONSTANTLY suffer from overheating devices, especially when it comes to iPhones, with streams often randomly crashing. Here's to hoping these new models fix that.

When it comes to aluminium making a comeback, I say THANK GOD. Titanium is nice, but it blemishes really freaking quick. I've had cases on my phone since purchase, and not cheap janky ones either, but well-built ones with REALLY nice construction down to the buttons even being extra tactile with beautiful clickyness to them. The issue is, take the cases off, and the buttons are permanently marked for no reason whatsoever. This NEVER happened with the aluminium models of yesteryear. Every iPhone I've ever had, was sold or traded in cosmetically as new when I was done with it. With the Titanium iPhone, that prospect goes out of the window as soon as you touch it. Honestly, in a fair world, Apple would have to pay me compensation for making the titanium phones so badly that this is an issue. It's literally a manufacturing defect, one which wouldn't ever be accepted on any other luxury item.

author: @GrantsPerspective
text: The amount of misinformation I see in the comments... Can we all please stop pretending that the Titanium model was "too hot" because of the Titanium? Stop falling for the marketing about "heat". They went with full Aluminum because it's cheap, and they had to make it sexy by making it "forged" and "unibody" and to make it different than the last gen iPhones. The ONLY thing that was Titanium in the old iPhone Pros were the side rails. If Aluminum is the answer for heat dissipation, the body of the iPhone 15 and 16 Pros were already made of it, so you could have just threw in heat pipes or a vapor chamber to dissipate heat across the whole phone. Instead, they just localized the heat dissipation to right where the mainboard was. The Titanium side rails are NOT the reason the phone got hot: Apple fricked up the thermal management in the already Aluminum body of the Pro 15/16 models, and turned adding a vapor chamber (which they should have already been doing) into a now fully Aluminum iPhone.

iPhone Air => 3149 mAh 
iPhone Pro => 4252 mAh 
iPhone Pro Max => 5088 mAh

author: @muhammedovet
text:
Hey, MrWhoseTheBoss team! I love your guys' productions, but I have one problem when a video has no music at the background. As a low-level, self-taught amateur editor, I may not have the qualifications to tell off a professional team, but just take it as a light constructive criticism.

The cuts in the audio really distrupt the flow and feel very off when the white noise that's being produced by the microphone just turns into complete silence.

I would recommend exploring just having the white noise from the microphone as an audio clip, and putting it on the spots where there are audio cuts. I feel like that would solve the feeling of stopping and starting the video constantly.

Although there might still be a slight cut feeling due to the reverb from the room you guys were recording the video in, however, it should solve the majority issue.

All the best!

author: @Phony-baloney8
text:
Few things for Apple to review:  
1) No one is asking for a thinner phone. 
2) Roughly 99.9% of iPhone users use a case, thus efforts put on making a stronger frame with a scratch-less screen aren’t things that the majority of us care about— personally, I would rather spend six dollars on a screen protector, and $20 on a phone case instead of upgrading
3) Battery life, while important, satisfies the majority of iPhone users; most charged the phone every night and battery. Life doesn’t become a problem until years later when the battery decreases and the software improves
4) the majority of us don’t know that much about tech could care less about frame rates and a 120 Hz display; asking us to pay nearly $1000 for a brighter screen grab a lot of people
5) when it comes to A16 Bionic chips, M2, M3, M4, and M5 microprocessor chips—Apple, it seems like releasing new ones every year is doing more harm than good, at least in the sales sense; given how fast Apple adds a new processor chip to their device devices and computers, it’s almost as if users are waiting longer to purchase a new Apple product; I have an M2 MacBook Air, and the M5s are already out; and right now, I see no need to upgrade from my M2 and I will probably wait till the M10 comes out. (Just as inside, Apple makes their products incredibly well, and there is little need to upgrade to a new product from them every year, watch us every 3 to 4 years. Right now, for instance, I am still using my 2012 iMac, and even though I haven’t been able to upgrade a software for years now, it still works incredibly well and does 99% of the things I want to do.)

author: @УлянаКривенко-щ5ч
text: If you look at 20th century sci-fi contains predictions of how the internet can lead to either great benefits of horrible dystopian futures. Unfortunately there appears to be far more ways it can lead to bad outcomes than good. For example, consider the help computers of the Star Trek franchise,  with say, Hal of 2001, or skynet of the Terminator films.  The latter is now close to being a reality, the independent AI drones flying kill missions on the battlefields of the war in Ukraine. It would be very unwise to dismiss the prescience of Sci-fi media as simply frivolous popular entertainment. The technology that appears in sci-fi has a habit of becoming reality a decade or so later. Not least because the young potential engineers and scientists have grown up to make it happen. The smart phone is the embodiment of a combination of the commutator from Star Trek, and the electronic book in hitchhikers guide to the galaxy.

author: @johnj596
text:
I think one big point people are missing is that, aluminum may help heat dissipation to some degree, but it’s not the main reason for using aluminum on the pro. The vapor chamber really is what is making the big difference! and on top of it the previous pros have an aluminum subframe inside. The titanium was only on the edge. I think the main reason for using all aluminum on the new pro is to lower production costs along side letting them make a new design. They moved the titanium frame to the air to make a bigger profit as the air has one camera, smaller battery and less titanium than last years pros. It was more of a way to still offer titanium and also new features on the pro models while making significantly more money than doing it all in one product.  

In my opinion I dislike this idea and prefer one phone with all the features, functionality and battery life with a beautiful design and titanium chassis.

I also agree with the idea that the air is paving the way for next years folding iPhone which will most likely be made of thin titanium.

Thank you, Mrwhosetheboss, for showing the iPhone 17. It was just released a day ago, and you finally explained everything we were eager to know. The battery life is also improved and lasts longer than its predecessor, which is another step forward.

I’m curious — it’s awesome that black wasn’t included in this series, but the number of available colors seems to be decreasing.

author: @RobinGarg-l8z
text:
Honestly, this event left me really disappointed. Apple seems to have completely lost the vision that once made the iPhone exciting. Year after year, we get the same presentation about “new materials,” “stronger shields,” or “cinematic camera modes” that 99% of regular people will never use. Let’s be real—how many average users are out there filming professional movies on their phones? These features don’t improve daily life for most of us.

Meanwhile, competitors like Google Pixel and Samsung are actually pushing innovation—AI-driven features, real productivity improvements, foldables, smarter integration into daily workflows. Apple, a company worth billions, seems more focused on marketing aesthetics and camera hype rather than meaningful upgrades that regular users can appreciate.

There were so many things they could have done. While others are aiming  for 100x zoom, they could do it only 8x. Promoting their new iphone saying: '' This is the most powerful iphone ever yet and we built it from Ground up'' like it means something when their phone does not provide any useful features like others are doing.

It’s sad to see Apple, once a leader in innovation, now just coasting on its brand name. If they don’t have a vision of their own anymore, at least look at what others are doing and put the Apple touch on it. That’s what made them great in the first place—taking ideas and making them better. Right now, though, it feels like Apple is stuck in the past, and the spark that made the iPhone magical is fading away.

author: @benedikthoffmeister3394
text:
Fully agree! Apple must have realized that the two value propositions of the Pro started to contradict each other and can’t be reconciled in one single model – at least not in the foreseeable future. Apple split the two value propositions (looks and performance) of prior Pros into two distinct models. 

The Pro model finally becomes a pure Pro phone that doesn’t have to compromise on raw power due to requirements for exclusive materials, such as titanium, or thinness. It is “form follows function” taken to the fullest. 

The Air model becomes the new beauty in the lineup, creating desire to own the latest iPhone. Its design and material selection is more important than performance or battery life, while still being a more than capable phone for most people.

I highly appreciate that move. It worked well for the MacBook Pros 🙂

author: @plutonicattic7995
text:
"iPhone" and "Everything's changed" in the same title is absolutely insane to see. Time to see what the differences are.

Smaller bezels (im pretty sure this change has been in literally every iphone since the second one, but hey probably the biggest increase since removing the home button, nice.)
Anti reflective coating (literally where???? I think that model doesnt have it bro cause what?)
Better camera (I mean nice, I guess. Noticeable improvement. Better than usual.)
Rear+front camera recording (likely for facetime, later down the line. Could be a nice feature to have, can't complain.)
Square front camera (absolute W, first apple addition i've been genuinely convinced is good in every way. Not even how long it took cause nobody was really waiting on it. nice one Apple.)
Faster charging (did not elaborate but FINALLY bro they pulled off sub hour 0-100, the line they've avoided crossing for so many years now, another W addition, unlike the square front camera, all I can complain about is the time it took to get here.)
Extra battery on video playback (doubtful, if it did happen, its likely because they messed it up before and fixed it, calling it a new feature rather than a fix, no shot you can get 8 extra hours from optimization ABOVE what was standard. they had to be below standard before somehow.)
Better CPU (literally does not effect anything at this point, genuinely how do they keep getting away with 20% increases year after year while hardly upgrading other components? the cpu has at least 10x the speed of the iphone 7 with the ram only being 4x from what I've seen, and the ram was already an issue back then, how is it getting worse year after year)
Upgraded storage (BASE WAS 128???????????? WHAAAAAATTTTTTT??????????? I AM NOT GIVING ANY PRAISE TO THAT I DO NOT CAREEEEE)
Price (Suprisingly good deal. No shot it doesn't give 2nd degree burns while holding it especially given prior overheating issues, but given the spec sheet its only a slight downgrade, except ram which is a MASSIVE deal here, from a oneplus 13 base model for $100 cheaper, props, far better than usual.)

Air:
ew

Pro/Pro Max
Best they've made literally ever. Honestly im usually a massive stickler for price, but I cant even say anything. absolutely insane work, really just feels like its made to be a good product first, flex second. I legitimately will not make fun of my friends who buy this, which I usually do HEAVILY. Running cooler is such a big deal for mobile devices and it annoys me how people dont care because it isn't "flashy". ABSOLUTELY AMAZING WORK APPLE.

I will remain with android because I personally enjoy it more conceptually, but THIS is what i've wanted out of apple. A genuine COMPUTER, not a status symbol or "just a cell phone" because they've been kind of like that for a long while. These are computers, which is what they should be.

The air doesn't exist in my eyes. still ew

author: @hpd_hero
text: For people who are curious to why the phone is aluminium instead of titanium, and what might be the pros and cons. The new phone will be a bit heavier, and aluminium is also a cheaper material which will visibly be more ragged/cheaper. If the reasoning behind this is cost or performance is hard to know, but titanium is severely more expensive so I’d guess its a budget thing. The good thing though is that Aluminium has way better thermal conductivity (10 x more). This basically means that as the phone is very packed with hardware, and a new powerful CPU, all this heat will dissipate into the aluminium shell, and then dissipated into the air. Does this make your phone cooler to the touch? NO. It will most likely make it warmer, which is a good thing for your processor, as it means its moving the heat away from it, instead of trapping it inside the casing. Technically, you holding your phone in your hands, or having it in your pocket will actually heat the phone up faster than it would have with the TI casing, but as your body temp is only 37 c it wont have a severe effect. Im happy they are prioritizing processing power, I personally couldnt care less if the phone is beautiful or not as I normally have a cover anyways. I think it will be a good release from Apple

author: @eanosh_
text:
I genuinely enjoy and appreciate most of your videos, Arun (Mrwhosetheboss). Your content is insightful, well-researched, and always engaging. However, when it comes to iPhone reviews, I can’t help but feel a sense of bias. It often seems like you hesitate to highlight the real drawbacks or call out the flaws as openly as you do with other brands.

As one of the top tech reviewers on YouTube, your opinions carry huge influence, millions of people rely on your reviews to make purchase decisions. That’s why it’s so important to be fully transparent, even if it means being critical of popular products. Balanced reviews that showcase both strengths and weaknesses not only build trust but also solidify your credibility as an unbiased reviewer.

Please continue to do what you do best, but don’t hold back when it comes to pointing out the cons, especially with Apple products. Your audience values honesty above all else.

All the best. Keep up the good work. 😊👍

Just a quick nerd side note.
Aluminum is lighter than Titanium however these phones are going to be slightly heavier (~7grams) than the 16&15 pros.

author: @CaminoWorld
text: With the iPhone air, they could have simply raised the back to be flush with the camera bump and filled that space with a full size battery. But of course they prefer to sell you the battery add-on instead. So now you've got this clunky two-piece device instead of a single elegant unit. I never liked Tim Cook as CEO. He seems like an accountant with no imagination. Apple was at least two years ahead of everyone when he took the reins and now they are two years behind. There's no question he's been able to squeeze a lot of profit out of Steve Jobs accomplishments, but they are clearly not interested in the best user experience. If they were, the iPhone air would simply have a larger battery and a nice clean flush back and they could have made it a slightly different user experience by having a special software package that makes far more efficient use of that larger battery. Nearly every other flagship phone has 5,000 milliamp hours now. At least it does over in Asia. Take a 5,000 mAh battery and tweak the software for people who've never even played a video game on their phone, who simply use it for basic YouTube videos, texting, etc and you get a phone that will go 3 days on a single charge. Guess they never thought of that.

author: @Toxic_Waste92
text:
I wish the Pro’s were still titanium… I am a highway line painter and we use glass beads to make the lines reflective… the glass beads are small enough to get into the gaps in a case and make deep scratches, grooves and marks in aluminum.

I went with the pro models simply for the battery, camera and the stainless steel frame… but even the stainless steel was marked up by the beads! When I got the iPhone 16 Pro Max the titanium has been amazing! No marks whatsoever! Even with the cut out for the camera control on my case (I hate the button and the cutout lol) there’s no marks. Just a bit of dirt that wipes clean!

Hopefully they return back to titanium at some point…

Titatnium and ceramic are used for insulation purpose. In aerospace they are used for shielding outer body of rentry vehicle or space vehicles. This companies used them as marketing. 

Last time apple and Samsung used titanium materials to promote. But devices faced heating issues.

In iPhone 17pro introduction video - aluminum frame, vapour chamber cooling everything looks good suddenly ceramic back - raised a question how can insulation material can be used for heat dissipation. Only few types of ceramic can dissipate the heat. 

If apple used proper ceramic back no heating issues, else it will have heating issues again.

text: I was wondering why they focused on mentioning the portrait photos of the iPhone 17 air on their website and I mean, portraits without using the portrait mode and fake bokeh, just natural bokeh, but they barely tapped into the same topic of the other main cameras phone's capability on portraits and now I see why, I just noticed that the aperture of the 17 air is actually f/1.6, bigger than the f/1.78 of the pro models. I MEAN???? Why didn't they use a bigger aperture on the main and even on the tele lens of de pros? I know that the tele lens of the pros have a bigger sensor now, which, automatically will create a shallower depth of field, but they should've at least have used f/1.78 for the tele lens and f/1.6 for the main lens, instead of keeping the f/1.78 for the main and f/2.8 for the tele! With the 15 and 16 pro max, apple really came for the cropped sensor cameras with all the improvements integrated to the iPhones, the 17 pro max came even harder to the competition with professional cameras, but they could've literally check mate the use of cropped sensor cameras, but still, they keep on holding back tech that they already possess, just to have something new to release next year. COME ON APPLE, you don't have to come up with an iphone every single year, just come up with a banger with all the tech you have, that the competition will have to wait YEARS to get close to that and by then you guys release another banger!

But then again, Apple has always needed to only polish up on what others have already done. That's more than enough to garner them them the highest of praises, as if they re-discovered fire.

Also, glad to see iPhone finally has Samsung's dual record feature, that lets you record through your rear camera and on selfie mode. 
Maybe Aaron might not see a use case for it but it's really great for when you're on video call and you wanna show someone something, while also maintaining face to face interaction with them. You don't have to choose between either lens

author: @jamesendurance4919
text:
Some intriguing features this year – AirPods Pro look like a real upgrade worth considering. The Watch Ultra 3 though? Besides slightly better battery and SOS connectivity, it feels almost identical to the first Ultra.

Had to laugh at Apple bragging about 120Hz finally coming to the base iPhone 17 as if it’s a revolution 😂 They even claimed it’s “better for gaming.” Other brands have had this for years—what exactly stopped Apple?

And the whole “2x optical quality telephoto” thing is comedy. It’s literally just a digital crop from the 48MP main camera, no telephoto lens at all… yet Apple somehow calls it 4 lenses in one. Marketing wizardry at its peak.

The slim iPhone Air is definitely novel tech, but who exactly asked for thinner phones? With compromises in camera, performance, and battery life, it won’t be an easy sell in places like Asia and Africa. “All day battery life” but not sure what that will translate to in actual usage.

The A19 Pro chip shifts focus heavily to AI computing, with claims of “MacBook-level performance.” Would be nice if they said which MacBook they’re comparing it to. Meanwhile, going back to aluminium for the Pro models screams cost-cutting, even if Apple frames it as an “upgrade.”

Finally, a vapour chamber! Maybe the real reason for aluminium? If the claim of 40% improved sustained gaming over the 16 Pro is true, that’s nuts. But then again, “best battery life ever” is a term Apple has used for every iPhon Pro Max launched in the last 3-4 years. Just noted the eSIM models now pack bigger batteries than the Samsung Ultra for the first time.

On cameras: 18MP front, 48MP on all three rears, 8x “optical-like” zoom (with 40x digital 😂), and ProRes RAW video capture. Still the best phone for video, but not quite at the Chinese “ultra” level. No doubt DxOMark will still crown it #1 🙄.

Other bits: Ceramic Shield 2 on the front, ceramic shield at the back, anti-glare coating like Samsung Ultra, new woven cases, 2TB storage option, same pricing as last year, and the whole event shot on iPhone 17 Pro.

Solid launch overall, but let’s be real—Apple’s marketing team deserves its own award this year 😂

author: @ganbly
text:
@danielnoujaim8004 Apple is waiting for production costs to drop before purchasing folding displays from Samsung. Once they can get them at a lower price, they’ll integrate their own chips and branding, then sell the devices at a premium that ensures massive profits.

Don’t be mistaken, they’re not thinking about you first. Their priorities are profits and margins, followed by some brilliant marketing, and finally, a product presented as something you’ve never seen before.

They have the privilege to take their time, thanks to their highly optimized software, loyal fan base, and strong ecosystem. This gives them the luxury of not rushing into trends, like how they’ve never released a MacBook with a touchscreen, just to push you toward buying an iPad and a Macbook altogether....

It’s not exactly consumer-friendly, but that’s the strategy. And in the end, you’ll likely buy it anyway because that’s what they’ll offer you, while you patiently wait for the rest.

After all, even if Apple doesn’t always deliver the most innovative or feature-packed product on the market, what they do release is usually polished and well-refined...or at least, that used to be the case. Let’s see how things go from here.
